---
title: "Pr(obabilities), E[xpectations], and the Truth"
title-slide-attributes:
    data-background-image: images/probability.png
    data-background-size: fill
    data-background-opacity: "0.5"
author:
    - name: Roemer J. Janse
      orcid: 0000-0003-0059-872X
      email: R.J.Janse-5@umcutrecht.nl
      affiliations:
        - name: UMC Utrecht
format: revealjs
---

# Disclaimer {background-image=images/statistician.jpg background-opacity="0.3"}

::: {.fragment}
**I am not a statistician**
:::

::: {.fragment}
We want to apply probability so:
:::

::: {.fragment}
We forego a formal study of probability and instead focus on thinking about probability.
:::

## Agenda
- Conflating Probability and Reality
- Probability: Our Fake Best Friend
- Expectations Instead of Probabilities
- How Do We Probably Find the Truth?
- How Can We Make the Truth Probable?

# Conflating Probability and Reality

## What is Probability?
::: {.fragment}
A reflection of our uncertainty:
:::

::: {.incremental}
- *It will probably be...*
- *The likelihood that...*
- *There is a risk of...*
- *We have a chance to...*
- *I expect that...*
:::

# Probability vs. Reality
- What is probability
    simple definition of porbability, how it is limited
- Is probability even real 
    are there any true probabilities outside of the quantu mworld?
- Determinism and stochasticism
    what kind of system do we live in


# Probability: Our Fake Best Friend
- The world is too complex to know the future
    Thus we must guess it
    To guess well, we need experience, in the form of many observations
- Groups vs. individuals
    In the medical sciences, those observations are often individual people
    At the individual level, the probability does not exist, but on the group level it does

# Expecting more than Probabilities
- We can also expect values instead of events (variance)/expected value
- Distributions
    Visualising all those experiences forms a distribution
    Tells us where we can expect most people to be
- Sufficient samples
    Sampling is a case of maximising your experience

# Thinking about finding the truth
- Probabilities and Expecations help us think about the truth. But what truth are we working towards?
- Frequentism: one truth
- Bayesian: an updated truth

# Dealing with uncertainty about the truth

- Frequentism
    - Confidence intervals
    - P functions/values
    - Bootstrapping
    
- Bayesian
    - Credibility intervals
    - Priors & posteriors
    



# Second talk
- applied probability
- from tests to regression models
- regression models & more complex architecture
- data driven vs. knowledge driven
- causal vs. descriptive vs. diagnostic/prognostic
- frameworks (EQUATOR)
- programming (R, python; Julia)
